thinking
**Debugging Perl heredoc**

I'm looking into the Perl heredoc in single quotes to see how to use the escape sequences correctly. Right now, I've used `if ($line = /\\\\newlabel\\{([^}]+)\\}/)`, but I need to understand how the shell processes this. The regex in Perl might be overescaped, leading to V1 labels missing. I need to simplify this by ensuring I'm using `\\newlabel` instead of `\\\\newlabel`. I should run the correct script and verify that all labels are active.
codex
The false negatives were from my own verification regex overescaping `\newlabel`, not from the manuscript. I’m rerunning the label check with the corrected parser against the exact repaired label set.
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "perl <<'PERL'
use strict;
use warnings;
my %labels;
my @queue = ('main.tex');
my %seen;
while (my "'$f = shift @queue) {
  next if $seen{$f}++;
  open my $fh, '"'<', "'$f or next;
  local $/;
  my $txt = <$fh>;
  while ($txt =~ /'"\\\\label\\{(["'^}]+)'"\\}/g) { "'$labels{$1} = 1; }
  while ($txt =~ /'"\\\\input\\{(["'^}]+)'"\\}/g) {
    my "'$path = $1;
    $path .= '"'.tex' unless "'$path =~ /'"\\.tex"'$/;
    push @queue, $path;
  }
}
open my $v1, '"'<', '../chiral-bar-cobar/main.aux' or die "'$!;
while (my $line = <$v1>) {
  if ($line =~ /'"\\\\newlabel\\{(["'^}]+)'"\\}/) { "'$labels{"V1-$1"} = 1; }
}
for my $k (qw(thm:rosetta-swiss-cheese subsec:rosetta-swiss-cheese sec:derived-center def:resolved V1-thm:operadic-center-hochschild V1-thm:annular-HH V1-thm:topologization V1-conj:topologization-general V1-constr:sugawara-antighost V1-eq:bv-field-space-hol-cs V1-eq:brst-antighost V1-rem:sc-higher-genus sec:line-operators ch:ym-boundary-theory ch:ym-instanton-screening)) {
  print "$k'"\\t\" . (exists "'$labels{$k} ? '"'ok' : 'MISSING') . \"\\n\";
}

+\begin{theorem}[$\Ethree$-topological for deformation-quantised PVAs;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}
+Let $\cA$ be a conformal vertex algebra at non-critical level
+whose Li-filtration associated graded $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is a freely generated Poisson vertex algebra.
+Then $\Zder^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ carries an
+$E_3$-topological algebra structure independent of the complex
+structure of $X$.
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The Khan--Zeng construction~\cite{KZ25} attaches to any freely
+generated PVA a three-dimensional Poisson-vertex sigma model on
+$X \times \R$. The conformal vector upgrades the holomorphic-topological
+symmetry to fully topological~\cite[Theorem~4.1]{KZ25}.
+The general half-space BV theorem
+(Theorem~\ref{thm:general-half-space-bv}) quantises the Neumann
+half-space theory, producing a 3d bulk with boundary~$\cA$.
+Construction~\ref{constr:topologization} then gives the
+$E_3$-topological structure.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Scope of Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}]
+\label{rem:E3-top-free-PVA-scope}
+The free-generation hypothesis on $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is satisfied by all standard families: Kac--Moody algebras $V_k(\fg)$
+(class~G), Virasoro and $\cW$-algebras (classes~L and~C), and
+matter-coupled systems (class~M). The theorem therefore establishes
+$E_3$-topological structure for every conformal vertex algebra in
+the G/L/C/M classification at non-critical level.
+\end{remark}
+
 \begin{conjecture}[$\Ethree$-topological for general conformal chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusConjectured]
 \label{conj:E3-topological-general}
 \index{E3-topological algebra@$\Ethree$-topological algebra!general conjecture|textbf}
@@ -6473,6 +6507,10 @@
 the conformal vector is absent entirely, the derived center
 carries only the $\SCchtop$ structure, and the $\Etwo$ factor
 remains chiral rather than topological.
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}, this conjecture
+is open only for conformal vertex algebras whose Li-filtration
+associated graded is \emph{not} freely generated (e.g., the
+Moonshine module~$V^\natural$).
 \end{conjecture}
 
 \begin{remark}[Which chiral algebras admit a $3$d HT bulk?]

+\begin{remark}[Strategy for class~$M$ via DS transport]%
+\label{rem:class-M-DS-transport-strategy}%
+Strategy~C reduces the Virasoro global triangle to
+Hochschild--DS compatibility.
+The triangle already holds for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$, which lies
+in class~$L$.
+The Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction
+$V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)\to \Vir_c$ is a strong deformation retract,
+and the ordered bar construction commutes with DS by the result
+proved in the manuscript.
+If chiral Hochschild cochains also commute with DS, the triangle
+for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$ transports to the Virasoro algebra.
+The missing input is an HPL transfer for the internal Hom functor
+$\RHom_A(M,N)$ through the DS strong deformation retract.
+The ghost-number bottleneck that trivializes the DS coproduct,
+proved in the manuscript, acts on the same filtration and may
+force the transferred Hom to be strict as well.
+At fixed conformal weight this is finite-dimensional linear
+algebra: the SDR data are explicit, and one computes the higher
+homotopies of the transferred $\RHom$ weight by weight.
+\end{remark}
+

+\begin{theorem}[$\Ethree$-topological for deformation-quantised PVAs;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}
+Let $\cA$ be a conformal vertex algebra at non-critical level
+whose Li-filtration associated graded $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is a freely generated Poisson vertex algebra.
+Then $\Zder^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ carries an
+$E_3$-topological algebra structure independent of the complex
+structure of $X$.
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The Khan--Zeng construction~\cite{KZ25} attaches to any freely
+generated PVA a three-dimensional Poisson-vertex sigma model on
+$X \times \R$. The conformal vector upgrades the holomorphic-topological
+symmetry to fully topological~\cite[Theorem~4.1]{KZ25}.
+The general half-space BV theorem
+(Theorem~\ref{thm:general-half-space-bv}) quantises the Neumann
+half-space theory, producing a 3d bulk with boundary~$\cA$.
+Construction~\ref{constr:topologization} then gives the
+$E_3$-topological structure.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Scope of Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}]
+\label{rem:E3-top-free-PVA-scope}
+The free-generation hypothesis on $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is satisfied by all standard families: Kac--Moody algebras $V_k(\fg)$
+(class~G), Virasoro and $\cW$-algebras (classes~L and~C), and
+matter-coupled systems (class~M). The theorem therefore establishes
+$E_3$-topological structure for every conformal vertex algebra in
+the G/L/C/M classification at non-critical level.
+\end{remark}
+
 \begin{conjecture}[$\Ethree$-topological for general conformal chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusConjectured]
 \label{conj:E3-topological-general}
 \index{E3-topological algebra@$\Ethree$-topological algebra!general conjecture|textbf}
@@ -6473,6 +6507,10 @@
 the conformal vector is absent entirely, the derived center
 carries only the $\SCchtop$ structure, and the $\Etwo$ factor
 remains chiral rather than topological.
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}, this conjecture
+is open only for conformal vertex algebras whose Li-filtration
+associated graded is \emph{not} freely generated (e.g., the
+Moonshine module~$V^\natural$).
 \end{conjecture}
 
 \begin{remark}[Which chiral algebras admit a $3$d HT bulk?]

+\begin{remark}[Strategy for class~$M$ via DS transport]%
+\label{rem:class-M-DS-transport-strategy}%
+Strategy~C reduces the Virasoro global triangle to
+Hochschild--DS compatibility.
+The triangle already holds for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$, which lies
+in class~$L$.
+The Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction
+$V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)\to \Vir_c$ is a strong deformation retract,
+and the ordered bar construction commutes with DS by the result
+proved in the manuscript.
+If chiral Hochschild cochains also commute with DS, the triangle
+for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$ transports to the Virasoro algebra.
+The missing input is an HPL transfer for the internal Hom functor
+$\RHom_A(M,N)$ through the DS strong deformation retract.
+The ghost-number bottleneck that trivializes the DS coproduct,
+proved in the manuscript, acts on the same filtration and may
+force the transferred Hom to be strict as well.
+At fixed conformal weight this is finite-dimensional linear
+algebra: the SDR data are explicit, and one computes the higher
+homotopies of the transferred $\RHom$ weight by weight.
+\end{remark}
+

thinking
**Testing label existence**

I’m trying to understand why the label `exists $labels{"V1-thm:..."}` comes up as false after I added it. Maybe the string interpolation is playing a role here, like the hyphen causing it to think there’s a subtraction? In Perl, though, I would expect `$labels{"V1-$1"}` to concatenate correctly. I think I’ll check if the captured label `$1` from the regex could be localized and causing issues with the hyphen. I need to test it directly!
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "perl <<'PERL'
use strict;
use warnings;
my %labels;
open my "'$v1, '"'<', '../chiral-bar-cobar/main.aux' or die "'$!;
while (my $line = <$v1>) {
  if ($line =~ /'"\\\\newlabel\\{(["'^}]+)'"\\}/) { "'$labels{"V1-$1"} = 1; }
}
print exists($labels{'"'V1-thm:operadic-center-hochschild'}) ? \"yes\\n\" : \"no\\n\";
print exists("'$labels{'"'V1-thm:annular-HH'}) ? \"yes\\n\" : \"no\\n\";
print exists("'$labels{'"'V1-thm:topologization'}) ? \"yes\\n\" : \"no\\n\";
print exists("'$labels{'"'V1-constr:sugawara-antighost'}) ? \"yes\\n\" : \"no\\n\";
PERL" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar-vol2 succeeded in 52ms:
yes
no
yes
yes

+\begin{theorem}[$\Ethree$-topological for freely generated PVAs;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}
+Let $\cA$ be a conformal vertex algebra at non-critical level
+whose Li-filtration associated graded $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is a freely generated Poisson vertex algebra.
+Then $\Zder^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ carries an
+$\Ethree$-topological algebra structure independent of the complex
+structure of~$X$.
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The Khan--Zeng construction~\cite{KZ25} attaches to any freely
+generated PVA a three-dimensional Poisson-vertex sigma model on
+$X \times \R$. The conformal vector upgrades the holomorphic-topological
+symmetry to fully topological~\cite[Theorem~4.1]{KZ25}.
+The general half-space BV theorem
+(Theorem~\ref{thm:general-half-space-bv}) quantises the Neumann
+half-space theory, producing a $3$d bulk with boundary~$\cA$.
+Construction~\ref{constr:topologization} then gives the
+$\Ethree$-topological structure.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Scope of Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}]
+\label{rem:E3-top-free-PVA-scope}
+The free-generation hypothesis on $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is satisfied by all standard families: Kac--Moody algebras $V_k(\fg)$
+(class~G), Virasoro and $\cW$-algebras (classes~L and~C), and
+matter-coupled systems (class~M). The theorem therefore establishes
+$\Ethree$-topological structure for every conformal vertex algebra in
+the G/L/C/M classification at non-critical level.
+\end{remark}
+
 \begin{conjecture}[$\Ethree$-topological for general conformal chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusConjectured]
 \label{conj:E3-topological-general}
 \index{E3-topological algebra@$\Ethree$-topological algebra!general conjecture|textbf}
@@ -6473,6 +6512,10 @@
 the conformal vector is absent entirely, the derived center
 carries only the $\SCchtop$ structure, and the $\Etwo$ factor
 remains chiral rather than topological.
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}, this conjecture
+is open only for conformal vertex algebras whose Li-filtration
+associated graded is \emph{not} freely generated (e.g., the
+Moonshine module~$V^\natural$).
 \end{conjecture}
 
 \begin{remark}[Which chiral algebras admit a $3$d HT bulk?]

+\begin{remark}[Strategy for class~$M$ via DS transport]%
+\label{rem:class-M-DS-transport-strategy}%
+Strategy~C reduces the Virasoro global triangle to
+Hochschild--DS compatibility.
+The triangle already holds for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$, which lies
+in class~$L$.
+The Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction
+$V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)\to \Vir_c$ is a strong deformation retract,
+and the ordered bar construction commutes with DS by the result
+proved in the manuscript.
+If chiral Hochschild cochains also commute with DS, the triangle
+for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$ transports to the Virasoro algebra.
+The missing input is an HPL transfer for the internal Hom functor
+$\RHom_A(M,N)$ through the DS strong deformation retract.
+The ghost-number bottleneck that trivializes the DS coproduct,
+proved in the manuscript, acts on the same filtration and may
+force the transferred Hom to be strict as well.
+At fixed conformal weight this is finite-dimensional linear
+algebra: the SDR data are explicit, and one computes the higher
+homotopies of the transferred $\RHom$ weight by weight.
+\end{remark}
+

+   Fix: restored legacy alias labels only where the mathematical object is unchanged; rewrote the Vol I citations to the explicit `V1-*` namespace where the source itself says “Volume I”; replaced the stale YM/line-operator/self-dual call sites with current truthful chapter/proof wording; and removed the two malformed `\\eqref*` call sites by converting them to non-label prose citations.
+   Status: `FIXED`

**Investigating regex issues**

I’m looking into why `thm:annular-HH` might be missing from the external main auxiliary file. I grepped earlier and found something, but it’s not loading. Maybe it's due to how the regex is structured, especially since `$1` is reused after later iterations. I initially thought the assignment in the loop would be fine. Should I consider if the line in the aux file has nested braces affecting the regex? I definitely want to test this directly.

+\begin{theorem}[$\Ethree$-topological for freely generated PVAs;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}
+Let $\cA$ be a conformal vertex algebra at non-critical level
+whose Li-filtration associated graded $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is a freely generated Poisson vertex algebra.
+Then $\Zder^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ carries an
+$\Ethree$-topological algebra structure independent of the complex
+structure of~$X$.
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The Khan--Zeng construction~\cite{KZ25} attaches to any freely
+generated PVA a three-dimensional Poisson-vertex sigma model on
+$X \times \R$. The conformal vector upgrades the holomorphic-topological
+symmetry to fully topological~\cite[Theorem~4.1]{KZ25}.
+The general half-space BV theorem
+(Theorem~\ref{thm:general-half-space-bv}) quantises the Neumann
+half-space theory, producing a $3$d bulk with boundary~$\cA$.
+Construction~\ref{constr:topologization} then gives the
+$\Ethree$-topological structure.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Scope of Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}]
+\label{rem:E3-top-free-PVA-scope}
+The free-generation hypothesis on $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is satisfied by all standard families: Kac--Moody algebras $V_k(\fg)$
+(class~G), Virasoro and $\cW$-algebras (classes~L and~C), and
+matter-coupled systems (class~M). The theorem therefore establishes
+$\Ethree$-topological structure for every conformal vertex algebra in
+the G/L/C/M classification at non-critical level.
+\end{remark}
+
 \begin{conjecture}[$\Ethree$-topological for general conformal chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusConjectured]
 \label{conj:E3-topological-general}
 \index{E3-topological algebra@$\Ethree$-topological algebra!general conjecture|textbf}
@@ -6473,6 +6512,10 @@
 the conformal vector is absent entirely, the derived center
 carries only the $\SCchtop$ structure, and the $\Etwo$ factor
 remains chiral rather than topological.
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}, this conjecture
+is open only for conformal vertex algebras whose Li-filtration
+associated graded is \emph{not} freely generated (e.g., the
+Moonshine module~$V^\natural$).
 \end{conjecture}

+\begin{remark}[Strategy for class~$M$ via DS transport]%
+\label{rem:class-M-DS-transport-strategy}%
+Strategy~C reduces the Virasoro global triangle to
+Hochschild--DS compatibility.
+The triangle already holds for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$, which lies
+in class~$L$.
+The Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction
+$V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)\to \Vir_c$ is a strong deformation retract,
+and the ordered bar construction commutes with DS by the result
+proved in the manuscript.
+If chiral Hochschild cochains also commute with DS, the triangle
+for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$ transports to the Virasoro algebra.
+The missing input is an HPL transfer for the internal Hom functor
+$\RHom_A(M,N)$ through the DS strong deformation retract.
+The ghost-number bottleneck that trivializes the DS coproduct,
+proved in the manuscript, acts on the same filtration and may
+force the transferred Hom to be strict as well.
+At fixed conformal weight this is finite-dimensional linear
+algebra: the SDR data are explicit, and one computes the higher
+homotopies of the transferred $\RHom$ weight by weight.
+\end{remark}
+

+\begin{theorem}[$\Ethree$-topological for freely generated PVAs;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}
+Let $\cA$ be a conformal vertex algebra at non-critical level
+whose Li-filtration associated graded $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is a freely generated Poisson vertex algebra.
+Then $\Zder^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ carries an
+$\Ethree$-topological algebra structure independent of the complex
+structure of~$X$.
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The Khan--Zeng construction~\cite{KZ25} attaches to any freely
+generated PVA a three-dimensional Poisson-vertex sigma model on
+$X \times \R$. The conformal vector upgrades the holomorphic-topological
+symmetry to fully topological~\cite[Theorem~4.1]{KZ25}.
+The general half-space BV theorem
+(Theorem~\ref{thm:general-half-space-bv}) quantises the Neumann
+half-space theory, producing a $3$d bulk with boundary~$\cA$.
+Construction~\ref{constr:topologization} then gives the
+$\Ethree$-topological structure.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Scope of Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}]
+\label{rem:E3-top-free-PVA-scope}
+The free-generation hypothesis on $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is satisfied by all standard families: Kac--Moody algebras $V_k(\fg)$
+(class~G), Virasoro and $\cW$-algebras (classes~L and~C), and
+matter-coupled systems (class~M). The theorem therefore establishes
+$\Ethree$-topological structure for every conformal vertex algebra in
+the G/L/C/M classification at non-critical level.
+\end{remark}
+
 \begin{conjecture}[$\Ethree$-topological for general conformal chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusConjectured]
 \label{conj:E3-topological-general}
 \index{E3-topological algebra@$\Ethree$-topological algebra!general conjecture|textbf}
@@ -6473,6 +6512,10 @@
 the conformal vector is absent entirely, the derived center
 carries only the $\SCchtop$ structure, and the $\Etwo$ factor
 remains chiral rather than topological.
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}, this conjecture
+is open only for conformal vertex algebras whose Li-filtration
+associated graded is \emph{not} freely generated (e.g., the
+Moonshine module~$V^\natural$).
 \end{conjecture}

+\begin{remark}[Strategy for class~$M$ via DS transport]%
+\label{rem:class-M-DS-transport-strategy}%
+Strategy~C reduces the Virasoro global triangle to
+Hochschild--DS compatibility.
+The triangle already holds for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$, which lies
+in class~$L$.
+The Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction
+$V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)\to \Vir_c$ is a strong deformation retract,
+and the ordered bar construction commutes with DS by the result
+proved in the manuscript.
+If chiral Hochschild cochains also commute with DS, the triangle
+for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$ transports to the Virasoro algebra.
+The missing input is an HPL transfer for the internal Hom functor
+$\RHom_A(M,N)$ through the DS strong deformation retract.
+The ghost-number bottleneck that trivializes the DS coproduct,
+proved in the manuscript, acts on the same filtration and may
+force the transferred Hom to be strict as well.
+At fixed conformal weight this is finite-dimensional linear
+algebra: the SDR data are explicit, and one computes the higher
+homotopies of the transferred $\RHom$ weight by weight.
+\end{remark}
+

thinking
**Investigating null bytes**

I’m noticing that the main.aux file might contain null bytes, which is pretty strange. It seems like our scripts are reading this file line by line, but they might stop at those null bytes. It’s possible that a specific line, like `annular-HH`, has these nulls too. Since it might be in a weird format, using `strings` could be useful, but I think I’ll try `grep -a` for now. Let’s see how that works out!
exec

+\begin{theorem}[$\Ethree$-topological for freely generated PVAs;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}
+Let $\cA$ be a conformal vertex algebra at non-critical level
+whose Li-filtration associated graded $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is a freely generated Poisson vertex algebra.
+Then $\Zder^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ carries an
+$\Ethree$-topological algebra structure independent of the complex
+structure of~$X$.
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The Khan--Zeng construction~\cite{KZ25} attaches to any freely
+generated PVA a three-dimensional Poisson-vertex sigma model on
+$X \times \R$. The conformal vector upgrades the holomorphic-topological
+symmetry to fully topological~\cite[Theorem~4.1]{KZ25}.
+The general half-space BV theorem
+(Theorem~\ref{thm:general-half-space-bv}) quantises the Neumann
+half-space theory, producing a $3$d bulk with boundary~$\cA$.
+Construction~\ref{constr:topologization} then gives the
+$\Ethree$-topological structure.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Scope of Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}]
+\label{rem:E3-top-free-PVA-scope}
+The free-generation hypothesis on $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is satisfied by all standard families: Kac--Moody algebras $V_k(\fg)$
+(class~G), Virasoro and $\cW$-algebras (classes~L and~C), and
+matter-coupled systems (class~M). The theorem therefore establishes
+$\Ethree$-topological structure for every conformal vertex algebra in
+the G/L/C/M classification at non-critical level.
+\end{remark}
+
 \begin{conjecture}[$\Ethree$-topological for general conformal chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusConjectured]
 \label{conj:E3-topological-general}
 \index{E3-topological algebra@$\Ethree$-topological algebra!general conjecture|textbf}
@@ -6473,6 +6512,10 @@
 the conformal vector is absent entirely, the derived center
 carries only the $\SCchtop$ structure, and the $\Etwo$ factor
 remains chiral rather than topological.
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}, this conjecture
+is open only for conformal vertex algebras whose Li-filtration
+associated graded is \emph{not} freely generated (e.g., the
+Moonshine module~$V^\natural$).
 \end{conjecture}
 
 \begin{remark}[Which chiral algebras admit a $3$d HT bulk?]

+\begin{remark}[Strategy for class~$M$ via DS transport]%
+\label{rem:class-M-DS-transport-strategy}%
+Strategy~C reduces the Virasoro global triangle to
+Hochschild--DS compatibility.
+The triangle already holds for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$, which lies
+in class~$L$.
+The Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction
+$V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)\to \Vir_c$ is a strong deformation retract,
+and the ordered bar construction commutes with DS by the result
+proved in the manuscript.
+If chiral Hochschild cochains also commute with DS, the triangle
+for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$ transports to the Virasoro algebra.
+The missing input is an HPL transfer for the internal Hom functor
+$\RHom_A(M,N)$ through the DS strong deformation retract.
+The ghost-number bottleneck that trivializes the DS coproduct,
+proved in the manuscript, acts on the same filtration and may
+force the transferred Hom to be strict as well.
+At fixed conformal weight this is finite-dimensional linear
+algebra: the SDR data are explicit, and one computes the higher
+homotopies of the transferred $\RHom$ weight by weight.
+\end{remark}
+

+\begin{theorem}[$\Ethree$-topological for freely generated PVAs;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}
+Let $\cA$ be a conformal vertex algebra at non-critical level
+whose Li-filtration associated graded $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is a freely generated Poisson vertex algebra.
+Then $\Zder^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ carries an
+$\Ethree$-topological algebra structure independent of the complex
+structure of~$X$.
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The Khan--Zeng construction~\cite{KZ25} attaches to any freely
+generated PVA a three-dimensional Poisson-vertex sigma model on
+$X \times \R$. The conformal vector upgrades the holomorphic-topological
+symmetry to fully topological~\cite[Theorem~4.1]{KZ25}.
+The general half-space BV theorem
+(Theorem~\ref{thm:general-half-space-bv}) quantises the Neumann
+half-space theory, producing a $3$d bulk with boundary~$\cA$.
+Construction~\ref{constr:topologization} then gives the
+$\Ethree$-topological structure.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Scope of Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}]
+\label{rem:E3-top-free-PVA-scope}
+The free-generation hypothesis on $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is satisfied by all standard families: Kac--Moody algebras $V_k(\fg)$
+(class~G), Virasoro and $\cW$-algebras (classes~L and~C), and
+matter-coupled systems (class~M). The theorem therefore establishes
+$\Ethree$-topological structure for every conformal vertex algebra in
+the G/L/C/M classification at non-critical level.
+\end{remark}
+
 \begin{conjecture}[$\Ethree$-topological for general conformal chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusConjectured]
 \label{conj:E3-topological-general}
 \index{E3-topological algebra@$\Ethree$-topological algebra!general conjecture|textbf}
@@ -6473,6 +6512,10 @@
 the conformal vector is absent entirely, the derived center
 carries only the $\SCchtop$ structure, and the $\Etwo$ factor
 remains chiral rather than topological.
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}, this conjecture
+is open only for conformal vertex algebras whose Li-filtration
+associated graded is \emph{not} freely generated (e.g., the
+Moonshine module~$V^\natural$).
 \end{conjecture}
 
 \begin{remark}[Which chiral algebras admit a $3$d HT bulk?]

+\begin{remark}[Strategy for class~$M$ via DS transport]%
+\label{rem:class-M-DS-transport-strategy}%
+Strategy~C reduces the Virasoro global triangle to
+Hochschild--DS compatibility.
+The triangle already holds for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$, which lies
+in class~$L$.
+The Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction
+$V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)\to \Vir_c$ is a strong deformation retract,
+and the ordered bar construction commutes with DS by the result
+proved in the manuscript.
+If chiral Hochschild cochains also commute with DS, the triangle
+for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$ transports to the Virasoro algebra.
+The missing input is an HPL transfer for the internal Hom functor
+$\RHom_A(M,N)$ through the DS strong deformation retract.
+The ghost-number bottleneck that trivializes the DS coproduct,
+proved in the manuscript, acts on the same filtration and may
+force the transferred Hom to be strict as well.
+At fixed conformal weight this is finite-dimensional linear
+algebra: the SDR data are explicit, and one computes the higher
+homotopies of the transferred $\RHom$ weight by weight.
+\end{remark}
+

+\begin{theorem}[$\Ethree$-topological for freely generated PVAs;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}
+Let $\cA$ be a conformal vertex algebra at non-critical level
+whose Li-filtration associated graded $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is a freely generated Poisson vertex algebra.
+Then $\Zder^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ carries an
+$\Ethree$-topological algebra structure independent of the complex
+structure of~$X$.
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The Khan--Zeng construction~\cite{KZ25} attaches to any freely
+generated PVA a three-dimensional Poisson-vertex sigma model on
+$X \times \R$. The conformal vector upgrades the holomorphic-topological
+symmetry to fully topological~\cite[Theorem~4.1]{KZ25}.
+The general half-space BV theorem
+(Theorem~\ref{thm:general-half-space-bv}) quantises the Neumann
+half-space theory, producing a $3$d bulk with boundary~$\cA$.
+Construction~\ref{constr:topologization} then gives the
+$\Ethree$-topological structure.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Scope of Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}]
+\label{rem:E3-top-free-PVA-scope}
+The free-generation hypothesis on $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is satisfied by all standard families: Kac--Moody algebras $V_k(\fg)$
+(class~G), Virasoro and $\cW$-algebras (classes~L and~C), and
+matter-coupled systems (class~M). The theorem therefore establishes
+$\Ethree$-topological structure for every conformal vertex algebra in
+the G/L/C/M classification at non-critical level.
+\end{remark}
+
 \begin{conjecture}[$\Ethree$-topological for general conformal chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusConjectured]
 \label{conj:E3-topological-general}
 \index{E3-topological algebra@$\Ethree$-topological algebra!general conjecture|textbf}
@@ -6473,6 +6512,10 @@
 the conformal vector is absent entirely, the derived center
 carries only the $\SCchtop$ structure, and the $\Etwo$ factor
 remains chiral rather than topological.
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}, this conjecture
+is open only for conformal vertex algebras whose Li-filtration
+associated graded is \emph{not} freely generated (e.g., the
+Moonshine module~$V^\natural$).
 \end{conjecture}

+\begin{remark}[Strategy for class~$M$ via DS transport]%
+\label{rem:class-M-DS-transport-strategy}%
+Strategy~C reduces the Virasoro global triangle to
+Hochschild--DS compatibility.
+The triangle already holds for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$, which lies
+in class~$L$.
+The Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction
+$V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)\to \Vir_c$ is a strong deformation retract,
+and the ordered bar construction commutes with DS by the result
+proved in the manuscript.
+If chiral Hochschild cochains also commute with DS, the triangle
+for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$ transports to the Virasoro algebra.
+The missing input is an HPL transfer for the internal Hom functor
+$\RHom_A(M,N)$ through the DS strong deformation retract.
+The ghost-number bottleneck that trivializes the DS coproduct,
+proved in the manuscript, acts on the same filtration and may
+force the transferred Hom to be strict as well.
+At fixed conformal weight this is finite-dimensional linear
+algebra: the SDR data are explicit, and one computes the higher
+homotopies of the transferred $\RHom$ weight by weight.
+\end{remark}
+

-At genus~$0$ the bar differential is a coderivation of the bar coalgebra, and $d^2$ is determined by its cogenerator projection. At genus~$g \geq 1$ the fiberwise differential $\dfib$ with $\dfib^{\,2} = \kappa \cdot \omega_g \neq 0$ is \emph{not} a coderivation (the cross terms at interior splittings produce a factor-$2$ discrepancy; see Remark~\ref{rem:bar-ordered-primacy} of Vol~I). This cogenerator extracts a double residue:
+At genus~$0$ the bar differential is a coderivation of the bar coalgebra, and $d^2$ is determined by its cogenerator projection. At genus~$g \geq 1$ the fiberwise differential $\dfib$ with $\dfib^{\,2} = \kappa \cdot \omega_g \neq 0$ is \emph{not} a coderivation (the cross terms at interior splittings produce a factor-$2$ discrepancy; see Remark~\ref*{V1-rem:sc-higher-genus} of Vol~I). The cogenerator projection nevertheless computes the \emph{obstruction class}: $\dfib^{\,2}$ is determined by the cogenerator projection to the degree-$1$ component $s^{-1}\cA$. +\begin{theorem}[$\Ethree$-topological for freely generated PVAs;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}
+Let $\cA$ be a conformal vertex algebra at non-critical level
+whose Li-filtration associated graded $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is a freely generated Poisson vertex algebra.
+Then $\Zder^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ carries an
+$\Ethree$-topological algebra structure independent of the complex
+structure of~$X$.
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The Khan--Zeng construction~\cite{KZ25} attaches to any freely
+generated PVA a three-dimensional Poisson-vertex sigma model on
+$X \times \R$. The conformal vector upgrades the holomorphic-topological
+symmetry to fully topological~\cite[Theorem~4.1]{KZ25}.
+The general half-space BV theorem
+(Theorem~\ref{thm:general-half-space-bv}) quantises the Neumann
+half-space theory, producing a $3$d bulk with boundary~$\cA$.
+Construction~\ref{constr:topologization} then gives the
+$\Ethree$-topological structure.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Scope of Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}]
+\label{rem:E3-top-free-PVA-scope}
+The free-generation hypothesis on $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is satisfied by all standard families: Kac--Moody algebras $V_k(\fg)$
+(class~G), Virasoro and $\cW$-algebras (classes~L and~C), and
+matter-coupled systems (class~M). The theorem therefore establishes
+$\Ethree$-topological structure for every conformal vertex algebra in
+the G/L/C/M classification at non-critical level.
+\end{remark}
+
 \begin{conjecture}[$\Ethree$-topological for general conformal chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusConjectured]
 \label{conj:E3-topological-general}
 \index{E3-topological algebra@$\Ethree$-topological algebra!general conjecture|textbf}
@@ -6473,6 +6512,10 @@
 the conformal vector is absent entirely, the derived center
 carries only the $\SCchtop$ structure, and the $\Etwo$ factor
 remains chiral rather than topological.
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}, this conjecture
+is open only for conformal vertex algebras whose Li-filtration
+associated graded is \emph{not} freely generated (e.g., the
+Moonshine module~$V^\natural$).
 \end{conjecture}
 
 \begin{remark}[Which chiral algebras admit a $3$d HT bulk?]

+\begin{remark}[Strategy for class~$M$ via DS transport]%
+\label{rem:class-M-DS-transport-strategy}%
+Strategy~C reduces the Virasoro global triangle to
+Hochschild--DS compatibility.
+The triangle already holds for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$, which lies
+in class~$L$.
+The Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction
+$V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)\to \Vir_c$ is a strong deformation retract,
+and the ordered bar construction commutes with DS by the result
+proved in the manuscript.
+If chiral Hochschild cochains also commute with DS, the triangle
+for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$ transports to the Virasoro algebra.
+The missing input is an HPL transfer for the internal Hom functor
+$\RHom_A(M,N)$ through the DS strong deformation retract.
+The ghost-number bottleneck that trivializes the DS coproduct,
+proved in the manuscript, acts on the same filtration and may
+force the transferred Hom to be strict as well.
+At fixed conformal weight this is finite-dimensional linear
+algebra: the SDR data are explicit, and one computes the higher
+homotopies of the transferred $\RHom$ weight by weight.
+\end{remark}
+

 \label{subsec:circle-bar-chain-model}

+\begin{theorem}[$\Ethree$-topological for freely generated PVAs;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}
+Let $\cA$ be a conformal vertex algebra at non-critical level
+whose Li-filtration associated graded $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is a freely generated Poisson vertex algebra.
+Then $\Zder^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ carries an
+$\Ethree$-topological algebra structure independent of the complex
+structure of~$X$.
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The Khan--Zeng construction~\cite{KZ25} attaches to any freely
+generated PVA a three-dimensional Poisson-vertex sigma model on
+$X \times \R$. The conformal vector upgrades the holomorphic-topological
+symmetry to fully topological~\cite[Theorem~4.1]{KZ25}.
+The general half-space BV theorem
+(Theorem~\ref{thm:general-half-space-bv}) quantises the Neumann
+half-space theory, producing a $3$d bulk with boundary~$\cA$.
+Construction~\ref{constr:topologization} then gives the
+$\Ethree$-topological structure.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Scope of Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}]
+\label{rem:E3-top-free-PVA-scope}
+The free-generation hypothesis on $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is satisfied by all standard families: Kac--Moody algebras $V_k(\fg)$
+(class~G), Virasoro and $\cW$-algebras (classes~L and~C), and
+matter-coupled systems (class~M). The theorem therefore establishes
+$\Ethree$-topological structure for every conformal vertex algebra in
+the G/L/C/M classification at non-critical level.
+\end{remark}
+
 \begin{conjecture}[$\Ethree$-topological for general conformal chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusConjectured]
 \label{conj:E3-topological-general}
 \index{E3-topological algebra@$\Ethree$-topological algebra!general conjecture|textbf}
@@ -6473,6 +6512,10 @@
 the conformal vector is absent entirely, the derived center
 carries only the $\SCchtop$ structure, and the $\Etwo$ factor
 remains chiral rather than topological.
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}, this conjecture
+is open only for conformal vertex algebras whose Li-filtration
+associated graded is \emph{not} freely generated (e.g., the
+Moonshine module~$V^\natural$).
 \end{conjecture}
 
 \begin{remark}[Which chiral algebras admit a $3$d HT bulk?]

+\begin{remark}[Strategy for class~$M$ via DS transport]%
+\label{rem:class-M-DS-transport-strategy}%
+Strategy~C reduces the Virasoro global triangle to
+Hochschild--DS compatibility.
+The triangle already holds for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$, which lies
+in class~$L$.
+The Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction
+$V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)\to \Vir_c$ is a strong deformation retract,
+and the ordered bar construction commutes with DS by the result
+proved in the manuscript.
+If chiral Hochschild cochains also commute with DS, the triangle
+for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$ transports to the Virasoro algebra.
+The missing input is an HPL transfer for the internal Hom functor
+$\RHom_A(M,N)$ through the DS strong deformation retract.
+The ghost-number bottleneck that trivializes the DS coproduct,
+proved in the manuscript, acts on the same filtration and may
+force the transferred Hom to be strict as well.
+At fixed conformal weight this is finite-dimensional linear
+algebra: the SDR data are explicit, and one computes the higher
+homotopies of the transferred $\RHom$ weight by weight.
+\end{remark}
+

**Organizing occurrences**

I’m considering how to create a list that groups occurrences and counts them. It seems like a straightforward task. I should think about how to organize the data effectively so that the results are easy to interpret. Maybe I could use categories or tags to help with clarity. +\begin{theorem}[$\Ethree$-topological for freely generated PVAs;
+\ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\label{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}
+Let $\cA$ be a conformal vertex algebra at non-critical level
+whose Li-filtration associated graded $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is a freely generated Poisson vertex algebra.
+Then $\Zder^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ carries an
+$\Ethree$-topological algebra structure independent of the complex
+structure of~$X$.
+\end{theorem}
+
+\begin{proof}
+The Khan--Zeng construction~\cite{KZ25} attaches to any freely
+generated PVA a three-dimensional Poisson-vertex sigma model on
+$X \times \R$. The conformal vector upgrades the holomorphic-topological
+symmetry to fully topological~\cite[Theorem~4.1]{KZ25}.
+The general half-space BV theorem
+(Theorem~\ref{thm:general-half-space-bv}) quantises the Neumann
+half-space theory, producing a $3$d bulk with boundary~$\cA$.
+Construction~\ref{constr:topologization} then gives the
+$\Ethree$-topological structure.
+\end{proof}
+
+\begin{remark}[Scope of Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}]
+\label{rem:E3-top-free-PVA-scope}
+The free-generation hypothesis on $\operatorname{gr}_{\mathrm{Li}}(\cA)$
+is satisfied by all standard families: Kac--Moody algebras $V_k(\fg)$
+(class~G), Virasoro and $\cW$-algebras (classes~L and~C), and
+matter-coupled systems (class~M). The theorem therefore establishes
+$\Ethree$-topological structure for every conformal vertex algebra in
+the G/L/C/M classification at non-critical level.
+\end{remark}
+
 \begin{conjecture}[$\Ethree$-topological for general conformal chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusConjectured]
 \label{conj:E3-topological-general}
 \index{E3-topological algebra@$\Ethree$-topological algebra!general conjecture|textbf}
@@ -6473,6 +6512,10 @@
 the conformal vector is absent entirely, the derived center
 carries only the $\SCchtop$ structure, and the $\Etwo$ factor
 remains chiral rather than topological.
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}, this conjecture
+is open only for conformal vertex algebras whose Li-filtration
+associated graded is \emph{not} freely generated (e.g., the
+Moonshine module~$V^\natural$).
 \end{conjecture}

+\begin{remark}[Strategy for class~$M$ via DS transport]%
+\label{rem:class-M-DS-transport-strategy}%
+Strategy~C reduces the Virasoro global triangle to
+Hochschild--DS compatibility.
+The triangle already holds for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$, which lies
+in class~$L$.
+The Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction
+$V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)\to \Vir_c$ is a strong deformation retract,
+and the ordered bar construction commutes with DS by the result
+proved in the manuscript.
+If chiral Hochschild cochains also commute with DS, the triangle
+for $V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$ transports to the Virasoro algebra.
+The missing input is an HPL transfer for the internal Hom functor
+$\RHom_A(M,N)$ through the DS strong deformation retract.
+The ghost-number bottleneck that trivializes the DS coproduct,
+proved in the manuscript, acts on the same filtration and may
+force the transferred Hom to be strict as well.
+At fixed conformal weight this is finite-dimensional linear
+algebra: the SDR data are explicit, and one computes the higher
+homotopies of the transferred $\RHom$ weight by weight.
+\end{remark}
+

 \label{subsec:circle-bar-chain-model}
